# noaa-recon-api

> Open-source HTTP API serving archival GOES satellite imagery and NOAA Tail
> Doppler Radar (TDR) data as georeferenced map tiles, plus a raw-netCDF
> passthrough for client-side rendering. CORS-open, free to call from any
> web app. Token auth is opt-in per deployment (off by default) — see
> "Authentication" below.

Live base URL: https://joshmurdock.net/api
Local dev base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8000
OpenAPI schema (machine-readable, authoritative): {base}/openapi.json
Interactive docs: {base}/docs

## Authentication

Off by default — check whether the specific instance you're calling has
opted in (ask the operator, or just try an unauthenticated call: a 401
means it's required). When enabled: every endpoint below except
GET /v1/health needs `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (401 without one);
get a token from the operator (admin console → API management), there's
no self-service signup. Cached tile URLs under /cache/... are NEVER
gated even when auth is on (the JSON endpoint that renders them is the
gated one) since they're meant to be dropped straight into an <img>/
Leaflet layer that can't send a header. Full reference: API.md
"Authentication".

## Live endpoints

- GET /v1/health: Liveness check. Returns {"status":"ok"}.
- GET /v1/satellite/tile?time=ISO8601Z&band=13|9|7|5|3|2&satellite=goes-east|goes-west&cmap=default|abi13|abi9|abi7|abi5|abi3|abi2|bd|ir4|enhanced|nrl|grayscale[&center=lat,lon&dims=N&unit=nm|km&resolution_km=N]: Requests a georeferenced GOES tile for the nearest actual satellite scan to an arbitrary UTC timestamp (~10-min resolution). band=13 Clean IR, 9 Water Vapor, 7 Shortwave IR/"fire temperature", 5 Near-IR Snow/Ice, 3 Veggie/Vegetation-NIR, 2 Red/Visible (5, 3, and 2 are reflectance, not brightness temp). satellite=goes-east (default; GOES-16 2017-12-18 until 2025-01-14, then GOES-19) or goes-west (GOES-17 2019-02-12 until 2023-01-10, then GOES-18) auto-resolve to the right satellite for the date. Optionally pass center+dims to render a fast, high-detail box around a point instead of the slow/coarse full disk (native ~2km/px for most bands, 1km for bands 3/5, 0.5km for band 2; resolution_km coarsens it). cmap=default (the default) resolves server-side to the correct per-band standard enhancement — bands are NOT interchangeable (different physical quantities, different color conventions); always let cmap default rather than hardcoding one band's table for another. Async: first call returns {"status":"generating","key":...}; poll the status endpoint below until ready. Full param/response reference: API.md#get-v1satellitetile
- GET /v1/satellite/tile?time=ISO8601Z&product=sandwich|geocolor&satellite=...[&center=lat,lon&dims=N&unit=nm|km&resolution_km=N]: Composite products instead of a single band/cmap (band/cmap are ignored if product is given). sandwich = Band 13 IR modulated by Band 2 visible texture. geocolor = a SIMPLIFIED APPROXIMATION of NOAA/CIRA's day/night true-color+IR composite (synthetic true color from bands 1/2/3 by day, colorized Band 13 IR by night, blended by solar zenith angle) — not the official proprietary GeoColor (no city lights, no atmospheric correction). center/dims (bbox) supported the same as a single-band tile.
- GET /v1/satellite/status/{key}: Poll for a tile job's result. Returns status ready|generating|error|idle, and on ready: png_url (relative — prefix with the base URL), bounds (Leaflet-format [[lat_s,lon_w],[lat_n,lon_e]]), band or product, cmap, satellite, scan_start, and (for a bbox request) center, width_km, resolution_km. These fields are also present while status=generating|error, not just ready.
- GET /v1/satellite/colortable?cmap=default&band=13|9|7|5|3|2: Returns the exact color stops for a colortable, read from the same data the renderer uses (not a hardcoded copy) — use this to render a legend that's guaranteed to match the actual tile output. Bands 5/3/2 (reflectance) return a % ramp instead of temp_c stops.
- GET /v1/satellite/products: Discovery endpoint — every band/product this API can render (with kind: brightness_temp|reflectance, default_cmap, native_resolution_km) plus the exact UTC date range each satellite (GOES-16/17/18/19) actually covers. Use this instead of hardcoding band/cmap/coverage knowledge client-side.
- GET /v1/storms/years: Every year with at least one named Atlantic or East/Central Pacific storm on record — 1950-onward, unnamed depressions/invests excluded. Sourced from HURDAT2 (reconciled historical archive) plus NHC's operational ATCF b-decks to stay current through the storm happening right now; a nightly systemd timer keeps it updated.
- GET /v1/storms/{year}: Every storm tracked that year: [{name, basin, atcf_id}]. basin is AL (Atlantic), EP (East Pacific), or CP (Central Pacific).
- GET /v1/storms/{year}/{name}[?basin=AL|EP|CP]: Full best-track for one storm — every ~6-hourly fix: datetime_utc, status, category (Saffir-Simpson label for hurricanes), lat/lon (decimal degrees, negative=S/W), wind_kt, pressure_mb.
- GET /v1/storms/{year}/{name}/nearest?datetime=ISO8601Z[&basin=AL|EP|CP]: The single track fix closest in time to an arbitrary UTC datetime — feed a year, storm name, and datetime, get the closest relevant data point back. basin disambiguates the rare case of a name reused across basins in the same year (else 409 listing candidates).
- GET /v1/recon/years: Every year with at least one archived hurricane-hunter recon mission (1-second flight-level data, decimated to 0.2 Hz), 2011-onward. Crawled from NOAA's raw archive at seb.omao.noaa.gov; a nightly systemd timer keeps it current.
- GET /v1/recon/{year}: Every storm with missions that year: [{storm_name, storm_id, mission_count}]. Unidentified flights are grouped under storm_name "Training / Research".
- GET /v1/recon/{year}/{storm_name}: Every mission for that storm: [{mission_id, aircraft, tail_num, flight_date, start_unix, end_unix, obs_count, source_url}], chronological.
- GET /v1/recon/mission/{mission_id}: Full decimated track for one mission (mission_id alone is enough, unique across all years/storms) — obs is an array of [unix_time, lat, lon, wind_kt, wind_dir, sfmr_kt, alt_m] tuples (~7 fields), plus source_url pointing at NOAA's original full-resolution NetCDF file (600+ variables).
- GET /v1/recon/mission/{mission_id}/download: Streams (not redirects) NOAA's original full-resolution NetCDF file for this mission — use this for anything beyond position/wind/SFMR/altitude (attitude, airspeed, raw sensor channels, etc.), since /mission/{id}'s JSON only exposes the ~7 decimated fields.
- GET /demo/netcdf-three/: Static demo page rendering netCDF data client-side via Three.js/WebGL (netcdf-three library). Currently loads sample data only.
- GET / and /v1/admin/*: Login-gated admin console (cache stats/browsing/deletion, one-off queries, bulk timeframe prefetch, token/account management, login/usage logs, the auth on/off toggle). Not for general agent use — requires session login (POST /v1/admin/login) as a superuser or moderator account; token/account management and self-update are superuser-only. See API.md "Admin console"/"API management" sections if you're operating this deployment, not just consuming it.

## Planned endpoints (return 501 today, shape documented for forward compatibility)

- GET /v1/tdr/missions, GET /v1/tdr/sweep: Tail Doppler Radar data from NOAA's raw aircraft archive. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/services/tdr.py for the planned design.
- GET /v1/raw/netcdf: Server-side netCDF subsetting by center/dimensions for client-side rendering. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/routers/raw.py (the recon MET side already has this — see /v1/recon/mission/{id}/download above).

## Full documentation

- [API.md](API.md): Complete endpoint reference, response shapes, color table descriptions, and copy-pasteable integration examples in curl, JavaScript (Leaflet), and Python.
- [README.md](README.md): Setup, architecture, deployment, and contributor/agent notes (roadmap, conventions to keep, a documented reprojection bug already found and fixed).

## Integration quick-start (JavaScript)

```javascript
const data = await fetch('https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/tile?time=' + new Date().toISOString() + '&band=13').then(r => r.json());
// poll https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/status/{data.key} every ~3s until status is "ready",
// then: L.imageOverlay('https://joshmurdock.net/api' + data.png_url, data.bounds).addTo(map);
```

## Notes

- No enforced rate limit even when token auth is on. Avoid polling status faster than every 3s.
- Identical requests (same band/cmap/satellite/resolved-scan-time, plus center/width_km/resolution_km for a bbox request) hit a server-side cache — cheap to repeat.
- Prefer center+dims over full-disk when you know roughly where you need imagery: measured ~11x faster processing (1.3s vs 14s for a 500km box) and ~130x smaller PNG. The initial ~25MB NOAA S3 download per scan is unaffected by bbox vs full-disk (not yet optimized).
- resolution_km can't go finer than sensor native (2km for bands 7/9/13, 1km for bands 3/5, 0.5km for band 2) — finer requests are silently clamped up, not rejected.
- License: MIT.
